title: Ocean Springs Elks Club Mardi Gras Parade
content: The Ocean Springs Elks Club kicks off the local Mardi Gras parade season with a daytime parade through downtown Ocean Springs featuring floats, beads, and community spirit.

title: OS Krewe Unique Mardi Gras Parade
content: Krewe Unique brings creative and uniquely decorated floats through downtown Ocean Springs immediately following the Elks Club parade. A beloved local Mardi Gras tradition.

title: MKOPI Mardi Gras Parade — Vancleave
content: The MKOPI Annual Mardi Gras Parade in Vancleave is themed 'Boot Scoot Boogie' for 2026. The parade route starts at John's Bayou Road and ends at Paige Bayou Marine. A fun, rural Mardi Gras celebration for the Vancleave community.
venue: John's Bayou Road to Paige Bayou Marine

title: Krewe of Little Rascals Children's Mardi Gras Parade
content: An annual children's Mardi Gras parade featuring kid-decorated floats and family-friendly fun along the downtown area of Pascagoula. Perfect for all ages.

title: Ocean Springs Carnival Night Parade
content: The Ocean Springs Carnival Association presents its spectacular night parade through downtown Ocean Springs. Illuminated floats, marching bands, and costumed krewes create a festive atmosphere as beads and throws fill the air.

title: Jackson County Carnival Association Mardi Gras Parade
content: The Jackson County Carnival Association hosts its annual Mardi Gras parade through downtown Pascagoula. The parade route runs from Ingalls Ave. to Market St., Market St. to Jackson Ave., Jackson Ave. to Pascagoula St., and back. Features elaborate floats, beads, and community celebration.
address: Ingalls Ave to Pascagoula St, Pascagoula, MS 39567

title: Ocean Springs Fresh Market
content: A Mississippi State certified, producer-only farmers market open every Saturday, rain or shine. Shop for organic produce, homemade baked goods, plants, herbs, locally grown fruits and vegetables, dairy, and artisan products. Based on the model of great European food and plant markets.

title: St. Paddy's Day Celebration & Parade — Ocean Springs
content: Ocean Springs celebrates St. Patrick's Day with an all-day celebration featuring live music, green-themed festivities, food, drinks, and a festive parade through downtown at 1 PM.

title: St. Patrick's Party & Parade — Pascagoula
content: St. Patrick's Party and Parade in Downtown Pascagoula featuring a Main Street shop and bar crawl all day, live music from 4-7 PM, and the annual St. Patrick's Parade at 7:30 PM.

title: Ocean Springs Easter Egg Hunt
content: The annual community Easter Egg Hunt in Ocean Springs offers fun for children and families with egg hunts, spring activities, and seasonal celebrations.

title: Feed the Need Music Festival & Fish Fry
content: The annual Feed the Need Music Festival and Fish Fry returns to Ocean Springs. Enjoy live music from local bands including Biloxi Bacon, Carey Hudson, The Kat Miller Band, and The Terrapins. All proceeds benefit Lord Is My Help, a community kitchen serving those in need across Jackson County.

title: 33rd Annual Ocean Springs Spring Arts Festival
content: This two-day celebration highlights more than 200 local artists, makers, and growers showcasing paintings, pottery, jewelry, plants, herbs, and more. Enjoy live artist demonstrations, seminars, entertainment, and garden goods as downtown shops, restaurants, and galleries partner with the festival.

title: Pascagoula River Run 5K
content: A scenic 5K run/walk along the Pascagoula River, one of the last unimpeded rivers in the lower 48 states. This spring race draws runners from across the Gulf Coast. Enjoy post-race refreshments and awards at the finish line.

title: Spring Pilgrimage Home Tour
content: Tour some of Ocean Springs' most beautiful historic homes and gardens during the annual Spring Pilgrimage. This self-guided tour takes you through charming residential neighborhoods showcasing the town's unique architectural heritage, from quaint cottages to grand coastal homes surrounded by blooming azaleas and live oaks.

title: Sean of the South at The Mary C.
content: Sean of the South brings his beloved Southern storytelling show to The Mary C. O'Keefe Cultural Arts Center. An evening of humor, heart, and tales from the American South.
venue: The Mary C. O'Keefe Cultural Arts Center

title: Pascagoula Crawfish Festival
content: Pascagoula's annual Crawfish Festival fills Beach Park with the aroma of boiling crawfish, live music, and family fun. Local cook-off teams compete for bragging rights while attendees enjoy pounds of fresh crawfish, cold drinks, kids' activities, and entertainment.

title: Earth Day Beach Cleanup
content: Join volunteers across Jackson County for an Earth Day beach and waterway cleanup. Help protect the Mississippi Gulf Coast's beautiful shorelines and marshes. Supplies provided — just bring your enthusiasm for keeping our coast clean.
address: Front Beach Dr., Ocean Springs, MS 39564

title: An Evening with Sister Hazel at The Mary C.
content: Sister Hazel performs live at The Mary C. O'Keefe Cultural Arts Center in Ocean Springs. Known for their hit 'All for You,' the band brings their signature blend of rock, folk, and pop to the intimate Trent Lott Performing Arts Theatre.
venue: The Mary C. O'Keefe Cultural Arts Center

title: Moss Point Catfish Festival
content: The annual Moss Point Catfish Festival celebrates the community's love of fried catfish with cook-offs, live entertainment, arts and crafts vendors, and family activities. A beloved tradition that brings the whole community together in downtown Moss Point.

title: Fridays at the Fort
content: A free monthly Friday evening event at the Fort Maurepas stage featuring food vendors, live music, and cultural activities. Held the last Friday of each month from April through September under the new stage roof at historic Fort Maurepas.
address: Fort Maurepas Park, Ocean Springs, MS 39564

title: Gulf Coast Dragon Boat Races
content: Teams paddle dragon boats across the waters off Ocean Springs in this exciting spring event. Watch colorful 40-foot dragon boats race across the harbor while enjoying waterfront festivities, food, and live music.
address: Front Beach Dr., Ocean Springs, MS 39564

title: Free Flowin' Fest — Pascagoula
content: A spring festival in Downtown Pascagoula featuring live music, arts and crafts vendors, food trucks, and family fun in the heart of the Flagship District.

title: Jazz Fest featuring Los Lonely Boys at The Mary C.
content: Jazz Fest in Ocean Springs featuring Los Lonely Boys at The Mary C. O'Keefe Cultural Arts Center. An evening of soulful Texican rock, blues, and jazz in the intimate performing arts theatre.
venue: The Mary C. O'Keefe Cultural Arts Center

title: Taste of Ocean Springs Food Crawl
content: The annual Taste of Ocean Springs is a downtown food crawl where participants sample dishes from local restaurants, bars, and eateries throughout downtown Ocean Springs. A great way to experience the diverse culinary scene.

title: Armed Forces Day Celebration
content: Jackson County honors our military service members with an Armed Forces Day celebration. The event features a ceremony with guest speakers, a veterans recognition, live music, and family activities. Free and open to the public.

title: Memorial Day Ceremony
content: Pascagoula holds its annual Memorial Day ceremony to honor those who gave the ultimate sacrifice in service to our nation. The solemn ceremony features a wreath-laying, patriotic music, and guest speakers.

title: Red, White & Blueberry Festival
content: A family-fun community event held in conjunction with the Ocean Springs Fresh Market featuring blueberry recipes, free vanilla ice cream topped with blueberries and strawberries, cooking demonstrations, vendors, and blueberry bush sales. A beloved summer tradition celebrating the local blueberry harvest.

title: Summer Concert Series at The Mary C.
content: The Mary C. O'Keefe Cultural Arts Center kicks off its Summer Concert Series with live performances on the lawn every Friday evening. Bring a blanket or lawn chair and enjoy local and regional musicians under the stars in beautiful Ocean Springs.
venue: The Mary C. O'Keefe Cultural Arts Center

title: Singing River Storytelling Festival
content: Named for the famous Pascagoula Singing River legend, this festival brings together storytellers from across the Southeast to share tales, folklore, and legends of the Gulf Coast. Enjoy an evening of captivating stories under the live oaks at the riverfront park.

title: Juneteenth Celebration
content: Moss Point celebrates Juneteenth with a community-wide event featuring live music, food, guest speakers, and activities for all ages. This event honors African American history and culture while bringing the community together in celebration of freedom and heritage.

title: Pascagoula Summer Music & Downtown Event
content: A summer evening event in Pascagoula's Flagship District featuring live music, art and craft vendors, and downtown shopping.

title: Fourth of July Celebration & Fireworks — Ocean Springs
content: Ocean Springs' annual Independence Day celebration on the eve of July 4th featuring patriotic festivities, live entertainment, food vendors, and a spectacular fireworks display at dusk over the Mississippi Sound.
address: Front Beach Dr., Ocean Springs, MS 39564

title: Fourth of July Celebration — Pascagoula
content: Pascagoula celebrates the Fourth of July at Beach Park with live entertainment, food vendors, kids' activities, and a grand fireworks finale over the Pascagoula River. Bring the family for a patriotic evening on the Gulf Coast.

title: Annual July Sidewalk Sale & Tax-Free Weekend
content: Two days of sidewalk sales throughout Ocean Springs coinciding with Mississippi's tax-free weekend. Local shops and boutiques offer special deals, discounts, and outdoor displays downtown, uptown, and all around town.
address: Government Street and surrounding areas, Ocean Springs, MS 39564

title: Back to School Bash
content: Jackson County families get ready for the new school year at the annual Back to School Bash. Free school supplies, backpacks, haircuts, health screenings, and community resources help families prepare for a great year.

title: Pascagoula Back-to-School Celebration
content: A community celebration of students and athletes at Pascagoula High School and Resurrection Catholic School as they head back to school. Family-friendly activities and community spirit.

title: Ocean Springs Artwalk
content: The annual Artwalk in downtown Ocean Springs features local galleries, artists, and creative vendors throughout the downtown arts district. Browse original artwork, meet artists, and enjoy the vibrant cultural scene.
address: Government Street and Washington Avenue, Ocean Springs, MS 39564

title: Pascagoula Blues & Heritage Festival
content: A celebration of blues music and Gulf Coast heritage in Downtown Pascagoula featuring live blues performances, local food, and cultural activities honoring the region's rich musical traditions.

title: Bud & Burgers Competition
content: A burger competition and craft beer tasting event at the L&N Depot Plaza in downtown Ocean Springs. Local restaurants compete for the best burger while attendees enjoy craft beers, live music, and the festive atmosphere.

title: Mississippi Songwriters Festival
content: The Mississippi Songwriters Alliance presents a four-day festival bringing 200 singer-songwriters to Ocean Springs and Gautier. Professional songwriters from Nashville and Mississippi share their craft through intimate acoustic performances at 10+ venues. All performances are free to the public.
venue: Multiple venues in Ocean Springs and Gautier

title: Vancleave Heritage Day
content: Vancleave celebrates its rural heritage with a day of old-fashioned fun. Enjoy tractor shows, cane syrup making demonstrations, quilting displays, live bluegrass music, and homemade Southern cooking. A celebration of the traditions that make this community special.

title: Pascagoula Hispanic Heritage Month Celebration
content: A community celebration of Hispanic Heritage Month in Downtown Pascagoula featuring live music, cultural performances, traditional food, and family-friendly activities honoring the Hispanic and Latino community.

title: Hummingbird Migration & Nature Festival
content: The annual Hummingbird Migration and Nature Festival at the Pascagoula River Audubon Center celebrates the fall migration of hummingbirds. Features certified bird banding demonstrations, conservation talks, bird walks, a petting zoo, local vendors, food trucks, kids activities, and nature education.

title: 49th Annual Zonta Arts & Crafts Festival
content: Pascagoula's premier arts and crafts show, now in its 49th year. Features over 200 booths spanning eight blocks in downtown Pascagoula with arts and crafts vendors, live entertainment, food trucks, and a free children's area. Proceeds support the Zonta Club's local charity efforts and student scholarships.

title: Cruisin' the Coast — 30th Anniversary
content: The 30th Anniversary of America's Largest Block Party. Car enthusiasts from 45+ states and multiple countries showcase antique, classic, and hot rod vehicles along a 30-mile stretch of beach-side highway with designated stops in Ocean Springs, Pascagoula, and other coast cities. Eight days of music, cruise-ins, sock hops, swap meets, and entertainment.
venue: Multiple venues along the Gulf Coast
address: Downtown Ocean Springs, MS 39564 (and venues coastwide)

title: Buffett & Bumpers — Pascagoula Cruisin' the Coast
content: A Jimmy Buffett-themed celebration during Cruisin' the Coast in Downtown Pascagoula, honoring Pascagoula's most famous native son with classic cars, island vibes, tropical drinks, and plenty of fun.

title: Cruisin' the Coast Sock Hop & Street Party — Ocean Springs
content: Ocean Springs' signature Cruisin' the Coast event featuring a sock hop and street party in downtown with live entertainment, classic cars on display, dancing, food vendors, and nostalgic 1950s-era fun.

title: Jackson County Fair
content: The Jackson County Fair returns to the fairgrounds with midway rides, livestock shows, demolition derbies, live entertainment, and all the classic fair food you can eat. From cotton candy to corn dogs, funnel cakes to fried everything — this week-long celebration has something for the whole family.

title: Gautier Mullet & Music Festival
content: A 35-year tradition celebrating coastal roots with fresh seafood, local vendors, live music, face painting, jump houses, a classic car show, a pet parade, and the legendary Mullet Toss competition. Free admission and open to the public.

title: Ocean Springs Rotary Run
content: An annual evening charity run/walk through downtown Ocean Springs organized by the Ocean Springs Rotary Club. Proceeds benefit local community projects and charitable causes.

title: Witches Ride of Ocean Springs
content: A whimsical Halloween event where participants dress in their witchiest attire, decorate their brooms, and ride through downtown Ocean Springs tossing treats to spectators. Also features a Halloween Pub Crawl (18+) and a Bewitching Pet Parade & Costume Contest. Since 2018, the event has raised over $115,000 for local animal welfare agencies.
address: Freedom Field to Government St, Ocean Springs, MS 39564

title: Trunk or Treat — Ocean Springs
content: A safe, family-friendly Halloween event where kids go trick-or-treating from decorated car trunks. Enjoy costume contests, games, candy, and fall fun for the whole family.

title: Ocean Springs Cemetery Tour
content: A guided historical tour of Evergreen Cemetery in Ocean Springs, bringing local history to life through costumed re-enactors portraying notable figures buried in the cemetery. A unique Halloween-season cultural event.

title: 48th Annual Peter Anderson Arts & Crafts Festival
content: The premier arts and crafts festival on the Mississippi Gulf Coast, now in its 48th year. More than 400 artists, crafters, and food vendor booths set up underneath the live oak trees of downtown Ocean Springs, surrounded by locally owned shops, restaurants, and galleries. Features live music, artist demonstrations, and family fun. Park & Ride shuttle service available.

title: Veterans Day Ceremony
content: Jackson County honors all who have served in the United States Armed Forces with a Veterans Day ceremony. The event features patriotic music, guest speakers, a rifle salute, and a wreath-laying ceremony.

title: Pink Friday — Shop Local Ocean Springs
content: A shop-local alternative to Black Friday held the Friday before Thanksgiving. Ocean Springs' locally owned shops, galleries, and boutiques offer special deals and holiday shopping events, encouraging residents and visitors to support small businesses.

title: Thanksgiving Day Turkey Trot 5K
content: Start your Thanksgiving morning with the annual Turkey Trot 5K run/walk through scenic Ocean Springs. This fun run draws hundreds of runners and walkers of all ages before the big feast. Costumes encouraged! Proceeds benefit local charities.

title: Pascagoula Christmas Parade
content: The annual Pascagoula Christmas Parade through downtown featuring holiday-themed floats, marching bands, photos with Santa, performances, and family fun. A cherished holiday tradition for the community.

title: Silent Light Festival
content: An immersive holiday arts event that transforms the Walter Anderson Museum of Art and downtown Ocean Springs streets into a glittering wonderland. Features multimedia art installations, a silent disco with dueling DJs, animated projections of Walter Anderson's designs, performers, dancers, and local food and drink.

title: Discover Christmas Parade — Ocean Springs
content: Ocean Springs' annual Christmas parade through downtown featuring holiday floats, marching bands, Santa Claus, and festive community celebration. Kicks off the holiday season in the City of Discovery.

title: Gautier Christmas Parade
content: Gautier celebrates the holiday season with its annual Christmas parade along Highway 90. Local schools, churches, businesses, and organizations create festive floats while Santa waves to excited children along the route.

title: Moss Point Christmas Parade
content: Moss Point lights up for the holidays with its annual Christmas parade through the heart of the city. Enjoy beautifully decorated floats, music, community spirit, and a visit from Santa Claus.

title: Walter Anderson Museum Holiday Art Market
content: The Walter Anderson Museum of Art hosts a special holiday art market featuring local and regional artists selling one-of-a-kind handmade gifts. Find pottery, paintings, jewelry, textiles, and more inspired by the Gulf Coast.

title: The Drop on Government — New Year's Eve
content: Ocean Springs' signature New Year's Eve celebration on Government Street featuring live music, entertainment, food and drinks, and a midnight countdown drop to ring in the new year in downtown Ocean Springs.

title: Pascagoula New Year's Eve Anchor Drop
content: Ring in the New Year in Downtown Pascagoula with live music, a light show, and the annual Anchor Drop off City Centre at midnight.
